name: bEASTside Kitchen
tagline: Covid pop-up turned Hawaii Kai neighborhood gem — live music nightly, garlic shrimp that rivals the North Shore trucks.
mustOrder: loco moco — elevated but still local
parking: Lot on Kalanianaole Hwy; ample compared to town restaurants
talkStory: Founded by James Kiko and Grandma Nette as a roadside pop-up tent in Waimanalo in September 2021. Grew into a brick-and-mortar on Kalanianaole Hwy in Hawaii Kai. Live music every night except Tuesdays, bingo nights on 2nd and 4th Mondays — this is as much a community gathering spot as a restaurant. The east side location means zero tourists. Happy hour until 6pm. Open late (midnight most nights).
